title: Koeleria pyramidata
intro: Koeleria pyramidata, also known as Junegrass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Asia. The health benefits of Koeleria pyramidata include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which make it a potential remedy for various ailments such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria pyramidata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Koeleria pyramidata is traditionally used in herbal medicine and is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, commonly used to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Koeleria pyramidata is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the tribe Poeae, which is a large and diverse group of grasses. The genus Koeleria is a small group of grasses that are native to the Northern Hemisphere. Koeleria pyramidata is further classified into the subfamily Pooideae, which is one of the two subfamilies within the family Poaceae. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other grasses and its shared characteristics.
